name: The Ultimate Lemon Meringue Pie- No Weep
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp Unsalted chilled butter, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
6 tbsp Unsalted chilled butter, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour, salt, and sugar in a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Scatter butter pieces over the flour mixture, tossing to coat the butter with a little of the flour. Cut butter into the flour with five 1-second pulses. Add shortening; continue cutting until the flour is pale yellow and resembles coarse cornmeal with butter bits no larger than a small pea, about 1-second pulses. Turn the mixture into a medium bowl.
2. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons cold water over the mixture. Using a rubber spatula, fold the water into the mixture; press down on the dough mixture with the broad side of the spatula, folding the water into the mixture. If the dough will not come together, add up to 1 tablespoon more cold water. Shape the dough into a ball, then flatten it into a 4-inch-wide disk. Dust lightly with flour, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for 30 minutes before rolling.
3. Generously sprinkle the work area with 2 tablespoons Graham cracker crumbs. Place the dough on the work area. Scatter a few more crumbs over the dough. Roll the dough from the center to the edges, turning it into a 9-inch disk, rotating a quarter turn after each stroke and sprinkling additional crumbs underneath and on top as necessary to coat heavily. Flip the dough over and continue to roll, but not rotate, to form a 13-inch disk slightly less than 1/8-inch thick.
4. Fold the dough in quarters; place it point in the center of a 9-inch Pyrex pie pan. Unfold to cover the pan completely, letting excess dough drape over the pan lip. To fit the dough to the pan, lift the edge of the dough with one hand and press the dough in the pan with the other hand; repeat around the circumference of the pan to ensure the dough fits properly and is not stretched. Trim all around, 1/2-inch past the lip of the pan. Tuck 1/2 inch of overhanging dough under so the folded edge is flush with the lip of the pan; press to seal. Press thumb and index finger about 1/2-inch apart against the outside edge of the dough, then use the index finger or knuckle of the other hand to poke a dent on the inside edge of the dough through the opening created by the other fingers. Repeat around the perimeter of the pie shell.
5. Refrigerate until firm, approximately 30 minutes. Prick the shell at 1/2-inch intervals; press a doubled 12-inch square of aluminum foil into the pie shell; prick again and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
6. Adjust the oven rack to the lowest position and preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Bake, checking occasionally for ballooning, until the crust is firmly set, about 15 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees, remove the foil, and continue to bake until the crust is crisp and rich brown in color, about 10 minutes longer.
7. For the filling, mix the first four ingredients in a large, nonreactive saucepan. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, whisking occasionally at the beginning of the process and more frequently as the mixture begins to thicken. When the mixture starts to simmer and turn translucent, whisk in egg yolks, two at a time. Whisk in zest, then lemon juice, and finally butter. Bring the mixture to a good simmer, whisking constantly. Remove from heat, place plastic wrap directly on the surface of the filling to keep it hot and prevent skin from forming.
8. For the meringue, mix cornstarch with 1/3 cup water in a small saucepan; bring to a simmer, whisking occasionally at the beginning and more frequently as the mixture thickens. When the mixture starts to simmer and turn translucent, remove from heat. Let cool while beating egg whites.
9. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees. Mix cream of tartar and sugar together. Beat egg whites and vanilla until frothy. Beat in sugar mixture, one tablespoon at a time, until sugar is incorporated and the mixture forms soft peaks. Add cornstarch mixture, one tablespoon at a time; continue to beat meringue to stiff peaks. Remove plastic from filling and return to very low heat during the last minute or so of beating the meringue (to ensure the filling is hot).
